text.title: Per-Framework Decomposition Patterns
text.summary: Framework-specific extraction rules and antipatterns for Django, Rails, Laravel, and React.

rule.statement: In Django, extract selectors.py (pure queryset reads, no writes) and services.py (business logic, writes, side effects) from views.py. Keep views to URL-dispatch and HTTP layer only.
rule.rationale: Django Styleguide (HackSoft) pattern: fat views are the most common Django debt source. Service+Selector split gives agents a clear mental model: "does this touch the database? Is it a read or write?"

antipattern.description: Creating UserService.get_user_by_id() that just wraps User.objects.get().
antipattern.reason: Wrappers without behavior are noise. They add indirection without adding testability or clarity.

rule.statement: In Rails, use service objects for multi-step business actions only — not for trivial CRUD. Return Result.success(value) / Result.failure(error) not exceptions.
rule.rationale: Service objects that wrap simple ActiveRecord calls fight the framework. Result types let agents handle success/failure paths reliably without rescuing exceptions.

antipattern.description: Rails service objects for simple CRUD that ActiveRecord handles natively.
antipattern.reason: Adds ceremony without benefit. Use Rails idioms for standard CRUD; service objects for multi-step actions with side effects.

rule.statement: In Laravel, use single-class Actions (from laravel-actions package or plain invokable classes) for each business operation. Use Form Requests or typed DTOs as the input contract.
rule.rationale: Actions map 1:1 to business operations, giving agents a clear entry point. DTOs typed with constructor promotion prevent silent field drift in service signatures.

rule.statement: In React, extract a custom hook when: the stateful logic is used in 2+ components, OR the hook body exceeds 30 LOC. Do not split JSX into 3-line components — composition has overhead.
rule.rationale: Over-extraction fragments the component tree without reducing complexity. Hook extraction yields returns only when logic is genuinely reused or genuinely large.

antipattern.description: React custom hook used in exactly one component and under 30 LOC.
antipattern.reason: Inline the logic — a hook used once adds indirection without discoverability benefit.

section.title: Finding Decomposition Candidates
section.reference: Bash: top-20 longest Python app files
Node: list React components over 150 LOC
